From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Football league season

[Divizia B](/wiki/Divizia_B "Divizia B")

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Season | 1998–99 |
| Country | [Romania](/wiki/Romania "Romania") |
| Teams | 36 (2x18) |
| Promoted | [Brașov](/wiki/FC_Bra%C8%99ov_(1936) "FC Brașov (1936)") [Extensiv Craiova](/wiki/FC_Caracal_(2004) "FC Caracal (2004)") [Rocar București](/wiki/AFC_Rocar_Bucure%C8%99ti "AFC Rocar București") |
| Relegated | [Rulmentul Alexandria](/wiki/CSM_Alexandria "CSM Alexandria") [Vega Deva](/wiki/Vega_Deva "Vega Deva") [Nitramonia Făgăraș](/wiki/CSM_F%C4%83g%C4%83ra%C8%99_(football) "CSM Făgăraș (football)") [Baia Mare](/wiki/CS_Minaur_Baia_Mare_(football) "CS Minaur Baia Mare (football)") [Dacia Unirea Brăila](/wiki/ACS_Dacia_Unirea_Br%C4%83ila "ACS Dacia Unirea Brăila") [Unirea Dej](/wiki/FC_Unirea_Dej "FC Unirea Dej") |
| Top goalscorer | [Marian Ivan](/wiki/Marian_Ivan "Marian Ivan") (17 goals) |
| ← [1997–98](/wiki/1997%E2%80%9398_Divizia_B "1997–98 Divizia B")  [1999–2000](/wiki/1999%E2%80%932000_Divizia_B "1999–2000 Divizia B") → | |

The **1998–99 Divizia B** was the 59th season of the second tier of the [Romanian football league system](/wiki/Romanian_football_league_system "Romanian football league system").

The format has been maintained to two series, each of them having 18 teams. At the end of the season, the winners of the series promoted to [Divizia A](/wiki/Liga_I "Liga I") and the last three places from both series relegated to [Divizia C](/wiki/Liga_III "Liga III"). A promotion play-off was played between the runners-up of the series to decide the third team that promoted to [Divizia A](/wiki/Liga_I "Liga I").[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2)

### Renamed teams

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=1998%E2%80%9399_Divizia_B&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Renamed teams")]

*Electroputere Craiova* was renamed as [Extensiv Craiova](/wiki/FC_Caracal_(2004) "FC Caracal (2004)").

*Maramureș Baia Mare* was renamed as [FC Baia Mare](/wiki/CS_Minaur_Baia_Mare_(football) "CS Minaur Baia Mare (football)").

Rules for classification: 1) Points; 2) Head-to-head points; 3) Head-to-head goal difference; 4) Head-to-head goals scored; 5) Head-to-head away goals scored; 6) Goal difference; 7) Goals scored.  
(C) Champions; (P) Promoted; (R) Relegated

## Promotion play-off

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=1998%E2%80%9399_Divizia_B&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Promotion play-off")]

The 2nd-placed teams of the Divizia B played a match to decide the third team promoted to [Divizia A](/wiki/1998%E2%80%9399_Divizia_A "1998–99 Divizia A"). The match was played on neutral ground, on the [Cetate Stadium](/wiki/Stadionul_Cetate_(Alba_Iulia) "Stadionul Cetate (Alba Iulia)") in [Alba Iulia](/wiki/Alba_Iulia "Alba Iulia").

[Rocar București](/wiki/AFC_Rocar_Bucure%C8%99ti "AFC Rocar București") v **[UTA Arad](/wiki/FC_UTA_Arad "FC UTA Arad")**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 19 June 1999 | **[Rocar București](/wiki/AFC_Rocar_Bucure%C8%99ti "AFC Rocar București")** | **2–0** | [UTA Arad](/wiki/FC_UTA_Arad "FC UTA Arad") | [Alba Iulia](/wiki/Alba_Iulia "Alba Iulia") |
| 14:00 [EEST](/wiki/Eastern_European_Summer_Time "Eastern European Summer Time") ([UTC+3](/wiki/UTC%2B03:00 "UTC+03:00")) | [Vrăjitoarea](/wiki/Bogdan_Vr%C4%83jitoarea "Bogdan Vrăjitoarea")  20' [Strelțov](/w/index.php?title=Alexandru_Strel%C8%9Bov&action=edit&redlink=1 "Alexandru Strelțov (page does not exist)")  90' | [Report](https://liga2.prosport.ro/special/remember-rocar-uta-4235437) |  | Stadium: [Cetate](/wiki/Stadionul_Cetate_(Alba_Iulia) "Stadionul Cetate (Alba Iulia)") Attendance: 12,000 Referee: Sorin Corpodean ([Alba Iulia](/wiki/Alba_Iulia "Alba Iulia")) |
